danger_signals.visual_form: Imperial/authoritarian architecture, uniformed soldiers, military insignia in towns and dungeons
danger_signals.meaning: Zone under hostile government control; player faction opposition indicated

danger_signals.signal: Chemical weapons and magical arms race
danger_signals.visual_form: Corrupted environments, poisoned terrain, artificially darkened sky regions, magical distortions
danger_signals.meaning: World state deterioration; environmental hazard zones and escalating threat level

danger_signals.visual_form: Destroyed landscapes, collapsed structures, blackened skies, environmental corruption spreading
danger_signals.meaning: World-ending threat proximity; irreversible damage occurring to game world

danger_signals.visual_form: Fortified positions, barricades, combat-damaged terrain, enemy fortifications
danger_signals.meaning: Active conflict zone; heightened enemy encounter rates and stronger opposition

safety_signals.visual_form: Civilian NPCs, shops, inns, peaceful architecture
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone for healing, commerce, information gathering; no random enemy encounters

safety_signals.visual_form: Transportation hubs, stationary vehicles, travel destination markers on overworld
safety_signals.meaning: Safe passage corridors; strategic mobility between safe zones

safety_signals.visual_form: Open field maps with reduced visual threat indicators vs. enclosed dangerous spaces
safety_signals.meaning: Overworld traversal offers navigation safety; dungeons indicate mandatory conflict zones

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Uniformed soldiers, military equipment, authoritarian aesthetics
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Fortified bases, imperial architecture, government-controlled towns, militarized zones

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Ragtag resistance fighters, civilians with improvised gear, liberation-themed aesthetics
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Hidden bases, liberated towns, resistance strongholds, safe houses

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Magical auras, technological glows, arcane markers
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Magically-enhanced soldiers, technological constructs, esper-aligned entities
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Magical research sites, esper temples, magitek facilities, corrupted magical zones

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Relatively intact towns, functioning infrastructure, civilian populations visible, limited military presence in some areas
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs conduct normal business; lower enemy encounter difficulty; story-driven progression through towns

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Increased military fortifications, damaged structures appearing, sky darkening in certain regions, environmental corruption spreading
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased enemy encounters; NPCs express fear or urgency; town activities disrupted; resistance activity emerges

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Poisoned terrain, unnatural discoloration, magical distortions in environment, corrupted flora/fauna, apocalyptic sky effects
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Environmental hazard mechanics activate; new enemy types emerge; civilian casualties evident; mass displacement of populations

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Widespread destruction, blackened skies, collapsed civilization, fragmented world layout, desolate landscapes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Drastically reduced NPC presence; dungeons become primary locations; survival-themed encounters; world becomes fragmented/non-linear

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Environmental healing indicators, restored structures, return of civilians, sky clearing in liberated zones
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPC morale improves; new settlements appear; hope-themed dialogue; constructive activities resume

environmental_storytelling.example: Imperial military zones use geometric, ordered, authoritarian design vs. civilian towns use organic, varied, community-oriented architecture
Zones affected by chemical weapons or magical warfare show visual corruption (color shifts, structural damage) increasing in severity over time
Collapsed buildings, burnt structures, and abandoned settlements visually communicate which regions have experienced conflict
As apocalypse progresses, civilian population visibly diminishes in towns; refugee populations appear in safe zones
Clear sky in early game; progressive darkening/discoloration in conflict zones; complete blackness during apocalyptic phases
Magitek installations show visual corruption/instability; magical auras become darker/chaotic as arms race escalates

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Patrolling soldiers indicate early-stage conflict; heavily fortified positions signal escalated threat or strategic importance
Normal: conducting business, relaxed dialogue; Distressed: urgent tone, fear expressions, warning players of danger; Displaced: appearing in refugee camps or uncharacteristic locations
Natural enemies: standard combat patterns; Corrupted enemies (chemical/magical poisoning): altered visual appearance, enhanced aggression, new attack patterns
Dormant: hidden in temples, no visual aura; Awakened: glowing auras, active presence in world; Corrupted: chaotic appearance, unpredictable behavior
Operational: clear landing zones, available for use; Damaged: visual deterioration, limited availability; Inaccessible: blocked or destroyed (world fragmentation indicator)

notes: Final Fantasy VI uses a mature narrative framework communicating world-state through progressive environmental corruption, faction architectural language, and NPC behavioral/population shifts. The game's primary visual communication strategy manifests the dictatorship-vs-rebellion conflict through military vs. civilian spatial design, with the apocalyptic event serving as a narrative pivot that physically fragments and corrupts the world visually. Chemical weapons and magical arms race are expressed through environmental color/integrity degradation. The overworld/town/dungeon structure provides safe vs. dangerous zone communication without explicit UI markers—towns are implicitly safe, dungeons are implicitly dangerous, creating emergent visual syntax for risk.
